name: Cone-beam Breast CT Study
description: The dataset of breast CT scans using Koning system
details: The system uses a tungsten-anode mammography tube (RAD71SP, Varex Imaging) operating at 49 kV with a 1.4 mm HVL, and an amorphous silicon flat-panel detector (PaxScan 4030CB, Varex Imaging) with 768×1,024 elements (388×388 μm²). The nominal image matrix is 1,024×1,024, with slice count depending on breast size. Each voxel measures 273.43 μm³. There are 155 volumes in total—50 from Koning and 105 from the University of Arizona.

name: Albany Medical Center Deidentified ICU CT Dataset
description: A deidentified clinical CT dataset of critically ill ICU patients with associated demographic, clinical severity, outcome, and body composition measurements.
details: This dataset consists of deidentified CT scans from ICU patients treated at Albany Medical Center. Each CT volume is stored in a folder named by Subject ID, which directly corresponds to the Subject ID column in the accompanying metadata spreadsheet. The dataset includes ICU admission information, mechanical ventilation status and duration, ICU length of stay, 28 ICU-free days, and 180-day survival outcomes. Clinical severity scores (APACHE II, SAPS II, SOFA), demographic variables (age, sex, race), prehospitalization comorbidities, laboratory values at admission, and CT-derived body composition measurements are provided. Body composition features include pectoralis major and minor muscle areas, erector spinae muscle areas (postural muscle), subcutaneous adipose tissue area, vertebral body density (bone), and major vessel diameters. All data are deidentified, and multiple severity scores should not be used simultaneously due to collinearity, as noted in the data dictionary.
project: Deep Learning for Medical CT and Critical Care Outcome Analysis
